title: Praxelis karuaiensis
taxonomy: Praxelis karuaiensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Praxelis, which is a group of plants commonly known as the "tickseed sunflowers" or "tickseed daisies". The genus Praxelis is further classified under the subfamily Asteroideae, which contains a large group of flowering plants. Praxelis karuaiensis is native to South America, and its classification reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history. The species is likely to be closely related to other Praxelis species, which share similar morphological and anatomical features.
uses: Praxelis karuaiensis is reported to have medicinal properties and potential uses in traditional medicine, particularly in the Amazon region.
